context: Common pests, diseases, and nutrient disorders in Punjab (Pakistan) agriculture. Use as reference labels only. Not authoritative.

entries.label: Wheat yellow rust (Puccinia striiformis)
entries.symptoms: Yellow to orange pustules in stripes along leaf veins, more on upper leaves. Pustules powdery, rub off on finger.
entries.ipm: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid late sowing. Balanced nitrogen, avoid over-application.
entries.chemical: Tebuconazole 250 EC at 250 ml/acre, or Propiconazole 250 EC at 200 ml/acre. Spray on first appearance and repeat after 12 to 15 days if needed.

entries.label: Wheat brown rust (Puccinia triticina)
entries.symptoms: Small round orange-brown pustules scattered randomly on upper leaf surface, no striping.
entries.ipm: Resistant varieties. Time sowing early. Remove volunteer plants between seasons.
entries.chemical: Propiconazole 25 EC at 200 ml/acre or Tebuconazole 25 EC at 250 ml/acre.

entries.label: Wheat loose smut (Ustilago tritici)
entries.symptoms: Black powdery mass replaces grain in the spike. Spikes appear blackened then bare rachis remains.
entries.ipm: Seed treatment is the only effective control. Source clean seed from certified suppliers.
entries.chemical: Treat seed with Carboxin 75 WP at 2.5 g per kg seed or Tebuconazole 2 DS at 1.5 g per kg seed.

entries.label: Wheat aphids (Sitobion avenae, Rhopalosiphum padi)
entries.symptoms: Small green or black insects clustered on leaves and ears. Honeydew, sooty mold, stunted growth.
entries.ipm: Encourage ladybird beetles. Spray neem oil for early infestation. Avoid excess nitrogen.
entries.chemical: Imidacloprid 25 WP at 50 g/acre or Acetamiprid 20 SP at 60 g/acre when aphids exceed 5 per ear head.

entries.label: Maize stem borer (Chilo partellus)
entries.symptoms: Pinhole shotholes on young leaves, dead heart in seedlings, tunnels in stem, holes near nodes.
entries.ipm: Destroy crop residues. Trichogramma egg parasitoids. Light traps. Resistant varieties.
entries.chemical: Carbofuran 3G at 8 kg/acre into whorl 15 to 20 days after germination, or Chlorantraniliprole 20 SC at 50 ml/acre as foliar spray.

entries.label: Fall armyworm (Spodoptera frugiperda) on maize
entries.symptoms: Whorl damage, ragged feeding on leaves, frass (sawdust-like) in whorl, late stage feeds on cobs.
entries.ipm: Pheromone traps. Hand-pick egg masses. Neem at early stage. Trichogramma releases.
entries.chemical: Emamectin benzoate 5 SG at 80 g/acre, or Chlorantraniliprole 20 SC at 50 ml/acre, or Spinetoram 12 SC at 80 ml/acre directed into whorl.

entries.label: Maize downy mildew (Peronosclerospora sorghi)
entries.symptoms: Chlorotic streaks on leaves, downy white growth on underside in humid weather, stunted plants.
entries.ipm: Resistant varieties. Avoid waterlogging. Rogue infected plants early.
entries.chemical: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl 35 WS at 6 g per kg seed. Foliar Mancozeb 75 WP at 600 g/acre.

entries.label: Cotton bollworm (Helicoverpa armigera, Pectinophora gossypiella)
entries.symptoms: Circular holes in bolls, rosette flowers (pink bollworm), shed squares and small bolls, larvae inside bolls.
entries.ipm: Bt cotton varieties. Pheromone traps. Trichogramma. Destroy stubble after harvest.
entries.chemical: Emamectin benzoate 5 SG at 80 g/acre or Spinosad 240 SC at 60 ml/acre. Rotate modes of action.

entries.label: Cotton whitefly (Bemisia tabaci)
entries.symptoms: Tiny white insects on leaf undersides, honeydew, sooty mold, yellow leaves, CLCuV vector.
entries.ipm: Yellow sticky traps. Avoid nitrogen excess. Plant trap crops. Encourage Encarsia parasitoids.
entries.chemical: Diafenthiuron 50 WP at 200 g/acre or Pyriproxyfen 10.8 EC at 200 ml/acre. Avoid synthetic pyrethroids that flare whitefly.

entries.label: Cotton leaf curl virus (CLCuV)
entries.symptoms: Upward curling of leaves, vein thickening and darkening, enations on underside, stunted growth. Spread by whitefly.
entries.ipm: Resistant or tolerant varieties. Aggressive whitefly control. Remove infected plants. Avoid late sowing.
entries.chemical: No direct cure. Manage whitefly vector with Pyriproxyfen 10.8 EC at 200 ml/acre.

entries.label: Cotton jassid (Amrasca biguttula)
entries.symptoms: Leaf margins yellow then bronze and curl downward. Plant stunted, hopperburn.
entries.ipm: Hairy-leaf varieties. Conserve predators. Avoid synthetic pyrethroids early.
entries.chemical: Imidacloprid 25 WP at 50 g/acre or Acetamiprid 20 SP at 60 g/acre.

entries.label: Rice blast (Magnaporthe oryzae)
entries.symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions with gray centers and brown borders on leaves. Neck blast blackens panicle base.
entries.ipm: Resistant varieties. Avoid excess nitrogen. Balanced silica. Drain field briefly at tillering.
entries.chemical: Tricyclazole 75 WP at 120 g/acre at early heading. Repeat after 14 days if pressure persists.

entries.label: Rice brown plant hopper (Nilaparvata lugens)
entries.symptoms: Yellowing then drying patches (hopperburn). Sticky honeydew at base of tillers. Insects hop when disturbed.
entries.ipm: Drain field for 3 to 4 days. Avoid blanket pyrethroids. Conserve spiders and mirid bugs.
entries.chemical: Pymetrozine 50 WG at 60 g/acre or Buprofezin 25 WP at 400 g/acre directed at plant base.

entries.label: Rice stem borer (Scirpophaga incertulas)
entries.symptoms: Dead heart in vegetative stage, whitehead (empty panicle) at heading.
entries.ipm: Pheromone traps. Trichogramma at 50,000 per acre. Destroy stubble.
entries.chemical: Cartap hydrochloride 4G at 8 kg/acre or Chlorantraniliprole 20 SC at 50 ml/acre.

entries.label: Sugarcane top borer (Scirpophaga excerptalis)
entries.symptoms: Dead heart of top whorl, bunchy top in older canes, exit holes near growing point.
entries.ipm: Set treatment. Trichogramma. Detrash regularly. Burn stubble.
entries.chemical: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC at 150 ml/acre as soil drench, or Cartap 4G at 8 kg/acre.

entries.label: Sugarcane smut (Sporisorium scitamineum)
entries.symptoms: Long black whip-like emergence from top of cane, thin tillers, reduced juice.
entries.ipm: Hot water treatment of seed setts at 50 C for 2 hours. Resistant varieties. Rogue infected stools.
entries.chemical: Carbendazim 50 WP soak setts at 1 g/L for 10 minutes before planting.

entries.label: Citrus canker (Xanthomonas citri)
entries.symptoms: Raised corky brown lesions with yellow halo on leaves, fruits, twigs. Premature fruit drop.
entries.ipm: Prune and burn infected wood. Windbreaks to reduce splash spread. Avoid leaf miner (entry wounds).
entries.chemical: Copper oxychloride 50 WP at 500 g/acre every 14 days during wet weather. Add streptomycin 100 ppm for severe infections.

entries.label: Citrus greening (HLB, Candidatus Liberibacter)
entries.symptoms: Asymmetric leaf yellowing (blotchy mottle), lopsided bitter fruit, twig dieback. Spread by Asian citrus psyllid.
entries.ipm: Remove infected trees. Manage psyllid aggressively. Use certified disease-free nursery stock.
entries.chemical: No cure. Manage psyllid with Imidacloprid 25 WP at 50 g/acre or soil-applied Thiamethoxam.

entries.label: Citrus leaf miner (Phyllocnistis citrella)
entries.symptoms: Silvery serpentine mines in young leaves, leaves curl and distort, secondary canker entry.
entries.ipm: Prune to synchronize flush. Conserve Ageniaspis parasitoid.
entries.chemical: Abamectin 1.8 EC at 60 ml/acre with horticultural mineral oil during flush.

entries.label: Nitrogen deficiency
entries.symptoms: Uniform pale green to yellow on older leaves first. Stunted plants, thin tillers, slow growth.
entries.ipm: Soil test. Side-dress urea. Split nitrogen applications.
entries.chemical: Urea (46-0-0) at 50 to 75 kg/acre split, or foliar urea spray at 1 to 2 percent solution.

entries.label: Phosphorus deficiency
entries.symptoms: Purple to reddish tint on older leaves, especially in cold weather. Poor root growth, delayed maturity.
entries.ipm: Soil test. Band-place DAP near seed at sowing. Avoid surface broadcast.
entries.chemical: DAP (18-46-0) at 50 kg/acre at sowing, banded 5 cm below seed.

entries.label: Potassium deficiency
entries.symptoms: Yellowing and browning of leaf margins on older leaves, scorched edges, weak stalks, lodging.
entries.ipm: Soil test for K. Apply muriate of potash before high demand stages.
entries.chemical: Sulphate of potash (50-0-0-18S) at 25 kg/acre, or muriate of potash (0-0-60) at 25 kg/acre.

entries.label: Zinc deficiency
entries.symptoms: Interveinal chlorosis on young leaves, shortened internodes, rosette in citrus, white bud in maize.
entries.ipm: Apply zinc sulphate at land prep. Foliar correction faster than soil.
entries.chemical: Zinc sulphate 33 percent at 10 kg/acre soil, or foliar 0.5 percent solution at tillering.

entries.label: Boron deficiency
entries.symptoms: Hollow stems, distorted young leaves, cracked stems, shedding of squares (cotton), corky fruit.
entries.ipm: Soil test before applying. Toxicity range narrow.
entries.chemical: Borax (Solubor) foliar 0.25 percent at flowering and boll formation.

entries.label: Iron deficiency chlorosis
entries.symptoms: Interveinal yellowing on youngest leaves, veins remain green. In citrus, entire canopy may yellow.
entries.ipm: Improve drainage. Avoid lime-induced chlorosis. Soil pH below 7 ideal.
entries.chemical: Iron sulphate 0.5 percent foliar with citric acid, or chelated Fe-EDDHA soil applied.

entries.label: Cotton bacterial blight (Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. malvacearum)
entries.symptoms: Angular water-soaked spots on leaves bounded by veins, black arm on stems, boll rot.
entries.ipm: Acid-delinted seed. Resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation.
entries.chemical: Copper oxychloride 50 WP at 500 g/acre with streptomycin 100 ppm.

entries.label: Rice sheath blight (Rhizoctonia solani)
entries.symptoms: Oval grayish lesions on leaf sheath at waterline with brown border, lesions enlarge upward.
entries.ipm: Avoid dense planting and excess nitrogen. Drain briefly. Resistant varieties.
entries.chemical: Hexaconazole 5 EC at 400 ml/acre or Validamycin 3 L at 500 ml/acre at booting.

entries.label: Wheat karnal bunt (Tilletia indica)
entries.symptoms: Partial bunt: a few grains in head replaced by black powdery sori, fishy odor when crushed.
entries.ipm: Resistant varieties. Avoid irrigation at flowering if disease was present prior season.
entries.chemical: Seed treatment Carboxin 75 WP at 2.5 g per kg seed. Foliar Propiconazole 25 EC at flowering if pressure high.

entries.label: Sugarcane red rot (Colletotrichum falcatum)
entries.symptoms: Drooping yellowing crown, internal stalk red with white cross-bands, sour smell.
entries.ipm: Resistant varieties. Hot water treat setts at 50 C for 2 hours. Rotate crop for 2 years.
entries.chemical: Carbendazim 50 WP sett dip at 1 g/L for 15 minutes.

entries.label: Generic abiotic stress (drought, waterlogging, heat)
entries.symptoms: Wilting, leaf rolling, marginal scorch, premature senescence. No pathogen signs (no pustules, lesions, insects).
entries.ipm: Irrigation scheduling. Mulching. Avoid spraying during heat. Check for waterlogging or salinity.
entries.chemical: Not applicable. Address root cause (water, drainage, salinity).
